key_insight: A Bayesian sequential version of iterative quantum amplitude estimation can turn rare structural failures into measurable quantum amplitudes and estimate failure probability with uncertainty bounds.
engineering_relevance: This offers a potentially faster, statistically interpretable route for structural reliability analysis when failure events are too rare for efficient direct Monte Carlo simulation.
method: Encode failure as a binary lookup-table oracle over a stochastic ensemble.
Run iterative quantum amplitude estimation at multiple Grover depths.
Use Bayesian updating on the amplitude angle to produce posterior estimates and credible intervals.
evidence_signal: Evaluated on stochastic finite-element benchmarks (a 1D bar and an L-bracket), where the method reduced estimation error versus direct Monte Carlo under the same idealized oracle-query budget and matched maximum-likelihood IQAE accuracy.
watch_item: The approach depends on idealized oracle access, so practical benefit will hinge on realistic quantum hardware, oracle construction cost, and noise tolerance.
paper_title: Bayesian Sequential Quantum Amplitude Estimation for Rare-Event Structural Failure Probability
